title: Chemically modified oligonucleotides with improved delivery properties
abstract: The disclosure relates, in some aspects, to methods and compositions for targeting chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecules to a site of interest, such as the liver or tumor. In some embodiments, compositions and methods described by the disclosure are useful for treating cancers.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprising a guide strand of 18-23 nucleotides in length that has complementarity to a target gene, and a passenger strand of 8-16 nucleotides in length, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises a double stranded region and a single stranded region, wherein the single stranded region is at the 3â end of the guide strand, is 2-13 nucleotides in length, and comprises at least two phosphorothioate modifications, and wherein at least 50% of the pyrimidines in the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule are modified, and wherein the passenger strand is conjugated to one or more moieties selected from the group consisting of: tocopherol, docohexaenoic acid, and docosanoic acid. 2. A chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprising a guide strand of 18-23 nucleotides in length that has complementarity to BRD4, and a passenger strand of 8-16 nucleotides in length, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises a double stranded region and a single stranded region, wherein the single stranded region is at the 3â end of the guide strand, is 2-13 nucleotides in length, and comprises at least two phosphorothioate modifications, and wherein at least 50% of the pyrimidines in the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule are modified, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises at least 12 contiguous nucleotides of a sequence selected SEQ ID NOs: 1-44, including the modifications shown in Table 1. 3. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of claim 2, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises a sequence selected SEQ ID NOs: 1-44, including the modifications shown in Table 1. 4. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of claim 2 or 3, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises at least 12 contiguous nucleotides of the passenger strand and/or guide strand sequence set forth in BRD4-3, BRD4-5, 

BRD4-8, BRD4-10, BRD4-17, BRD4-18, BRD4-20, BRD4-21, or BRD4-22, including the modifications shown in Table 1. 5. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of claim 4, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises a sense strand having the sequence set forth in BRD4-3 (SEQ ID NO: 5), BRD4-5 (SEQ ID NO: 9), BRD4-8 (SEQ ID NO: 15), BRD4-10 (SEQ ID NO: 19), BRD4-17 (SEQ ID NO: 33), BRD4-18 (SEQ ID NO: 35), BRD4-20 (SEQ ID NO: 39), BRD4-21 (SEQ ID NO: 41), or BRD4-22 (SEQ ID NO: 43), and/or an antisense strand having the sequence set forth in BRD4-3 (SEQ ID NO: 6), BRD4-5 (SEQ ID NO: 10), BRD4-8 (SEQ ID NO: 16), BRD4-10 (SEQ ID NO: 20), BRD4-17 (SEQ ID NO: 34), BRD4-18 (SEQ ID NO: 36), BRD4-20 (SEQ ID NO: 40), BRD4-21 (SEQ ID NO: 42), BRD4- 22 (SEQ ID NO: 44), including the modifications shown in Table 1. 6. A chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprising a guide strand of 18-23 nucleotides in length that has complementarity to PD1, and a passenger strand of 8-16 nucleotides in length, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises a double stranded region and a single stranded region, wherein the single stranded region is at the 3â end of the guide strand, is 2-13 nucleotides in length, and comprises at least two phosphorothioate modifications, and wherein at least 50% of the pyrimidines in the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule are modified, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises at least 12 contiguous nucleotides of a sequence selected SEQ ID NOs: 45-83, including the modifications shown in Table 2. 7. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of claim 6, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises a sequence selected SEQ ID NOs: 45-83, including the modifications shown in Table 2. 8. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of claim 6 or 7, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises at least 12 contiguous nucleotides of the passenger strand and/or guide strand sequence set forth in PD1-3, PD1-5, 

PD1-10, PD1-16, PD1-17, PD1-18, PD1-19, PD1-20, or PD1-21, including the modifications shown in Table 2. 9. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of claim 8, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises a sense strand having the sequence set forth in PD1-3 (SEQ ID NO: 49), PD1-5 (SEQ ID NO: 53), PD1-10 (SEQ ID NO: 63), PD1-16 (SEQ ID NO: 75), PD1-17 (SEQ ID NO: 77), PD1-18 (SEQ ID NO: 79), PD1-19 (SEQ ID NO: 81), PD1-20 (SEQ ID NO: 79), or PD1-21 (SEQ ID NO: 77) and/or an antisense strand having the sequence set forth in PD1-3 (SEQ ID NO: 50), PD1-5 (SEQ ID NO: 54), PD1- 10 (SEQ ID NO: 64), PD1-16 (SEQ ID NO: 76), PD1-17 (SEQ ID NO: 78), PD1-18 (SEQ ID NO: 80), PD1-19 (SEQ ID NO: 82), PD1-20 (SEQ ID NO: 83), or PD1-21 (SEQ ID NO: 83), including the modifications shown in Table 2. 10. A chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprising a guide strand of 18-23 nucleotides in length that has complementarity to TIGIT, and a passenger strand of 8-16 nucleotides in length, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises a double stranded region and a single stranded region, wherein the single stranded region is at the 3â end of the guide strand, is 2-13 nucleotides in length, and comprises at least two phosphorothioate modifications, and wherein at least 50% of the pyrimidines in the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule are modified, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises at least 12 contiguous nucleotides of a sequence selected SEQ ID NOs: 84-87, including the modifications shown in Table 4. 11. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of claim 10, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises a sequence selected SEQ ID NOs: 84-87, including the modifications shown in Table 4. 12. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of claim 10 or 11, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises at least 12 contiguous nucleotides of the passenger strand and/or guide strand sequence set forth in TIGIT- 1, TIGIT-2, or TIGIT-3, including the modifications shown in Table 4. 

 - 127 - 13. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of claim 12, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule comprises a sense strand having the sequence set forth in TIGIT-1 (SEQ ID NO: 84), TIGIT-2 (SEQ ID NO: 86) or TIGIT-3 (SEQ ID NO: 87), and/or an antisense strand having the sequence set forth in TIGIT-1 (SEQ ID NO: 85), TIGIT-2 (SEQ ID NO: 85) or TIGIT-3 (SEQ ID NO: 85), including the modifications shown in Table 4. 14. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 2 to 13, further comprising one or more N-acetyl glucosamine (GalNac) moieties, optionally wherein the one or more GalNac moieties are conjugated to the 3â end of the passenger strand. 15. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 2 to 14, further comprising one or more tocopherol moieties, optionally wherein the one or more tocopherol moieties are conjugated to the 3â end of the passenger strand. 16. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 2 to 15, further comprising one or more docohexaenoic acid moieties, optionally wherein the one or more docohexaenoic acid moieties are conjugated to the 3â end of the passenger strand. 17. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 2 to 16, further comprising one or more docosanoic acid moieties, optionally wherein the one or more docosanoic acid moieties are conjugated to the 3â end of the passenger strand. 18. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 2 to 17, further comprising one or more cholesterol moieties, optionally wherein the one or more cholesterol moieties are conjugated to the 3â end of the passenger strand. 19. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule is an INTAS YLâ¢ molecule and wherein the INTASYLâ¢ molecule is conjugated to 1-4 N-acetyl glucosamine 

 - 128 - (GalNac) targeting moieties, 1-4 tocopherol moieties, 1-4 docohexaenoic acid moieties, 1-4 docosanoic acid moieties, cholesterol, or any combination thereof. 20. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein the first nucleotide relative to the 5 âend of the guide strand has a 2'-O-methyl modification, optionally wherein the 2'-O-methyl modification is a 5P-2'-O-methyl U modification, or a 5â vinyl phosphonate 2â-O-methyl U modification. 21. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein at least 60%, at least 80%, at least 90% or wherein 100% of the pyrimidines in the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule are modified. 22. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 1 to 21, wherein the modified pyrimidines are 2'-fluoro or 2'-O-methyl modified. 23. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 1 to 22, wherein at least one U or C includes a hydrophobic modification, optionally wherein a plurality of U's and/or C's include a hydrophobic modification. 24. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of claim 23, wherein the hydrophobic modification is a methyl or ethyl hydrophobic base modification. 25. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 1 to 24, wherein the guide strand comprises 4-10 phosphorothioate modifications. 26. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of claim 25, wherein the guide strand comprises at least six phosphorothioate modifications located within the first 10 nucleotides relative to the 3 âend of the guide strand. 27. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 1 to 26, wherein the guide strand includes 4-14 phosphate modifications. 

 - 129 - 28. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 1 to 27, wherein the single stranded region of the guide strand is 2 nucleotides long to 8 nucleotides long. 29. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 1 to 28, wherein the double stranded region is 14 or 15 or 16 or 17 or 18 nucleotides long. 30. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 1 to 29, wherein the double stranded nucleic acid molecule has one end that is blunt or includes a one nucleotide overhang. 31. The chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 1 to 30, wherein the chemistry and backbone is selected from Table 3 for the antisense and the sense strand. 32. A composition comprising the chemically-modified double stranded nucleic acid molecule of any one of claims 1 to 31 and a pharmaceutically acceptable excipient. 33. A method for treating a subject suffering from a proliferative disease or a disease of the liver, the method comprising administering the composition of claim 32. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the proliferative disease is cancer. 35. The method of claim 34, wherein the cancer is selected from the group consisting of: head and neck, lung cancer, breast cancer, melanoma, cervical cancer, hepatocellular carcinoma, hepatoblastoma, cholangiocarcinoma, and liver angiosarcoma. 36. The method of any one of claims 33 to 35, wherein the composition is administered via systemic injection. 37. The method of any one of claims 33 to 36, wherein the composition is administered via local administration. 

 - 130 - 38. The method of any one of claims 33 to 35, comprising administering the composition ex vivo to a population of cells. 39. The method of claim 38, wherein the population of cells is from the subject. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein the population of cells is from a healthy second subject. 41. The method of any one of claims 38 to 40, wherein the population of cells comprises immune cells. 42. The method of claim 41, wherein the immune cells comprise T cells, natural killer (NK) cells, B cells, or combinations thereof. 43. The method of any one of claims 38 to 42, further comprising administering the population of cells comprising the composition to the subject.
